mnemonics.title: IoT Security Pillars
mnemonics.content: CIA - Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability - Remember: 'Confidential Information Always Available' to recall the core security goals.

mnemonics.title: IoT Attack Types
mnemonics.content: KRA Blue - KRACK, Replay attacks, BlueBorne - Mnemonic: 'Keep Researching And Learn About BlueBorne' for common wireless IoT attacks.

mnemonics.title: Secure IoT Checklist
mnemonics.content: P-A-S-S-W-D - Patches, Access control, Segmentation, Strong passwords, Watch traffic, Disable unused services

acronyms.usage: Exploits WPA2 4-way handshake by reinstalling encryption keys
Web protocol for constrained IoT devices
Hardware security chip for cryptographic key storage
Remote software/firmware updates for IoT devices
Attack using botnet to overwhelm target systems

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a secure handshake between two people where they exchange keys, lock boxes, and verify identity at each step. KRACK attacks by intercepting and replaying the key exchange.
Picture your home network as a building with different rooms. The guest WiFi is a separate room - even if intruders get in, they cannot access the main living areas (your secure devices).
Imagine thousands of innocent IoT devices as 'zombie' followers that a hacker commands like a dictator to attack a website simultaneously.

analogies.analogy: Like leaving your house key under the doormat - attackers know exactly where to look and can easily enter.
Like getting vaccine boosters - they update your body's protection against new threats.
Like having separate lifeboats on a ship - if one is compromised, others remain safe.
Like both a bouncer checking your ID AND you verifying the bouncer is legitimate - both parties must prove identity.
Like speaking a secret language only you and the recipient understand - even if someone overhears, they cannot understand.
